title: Implement canonical event-to-state synchronization for transaction progression
summary: Implement the Phase 2 runtime changes for v0.4.12 so canonical transaction events and materialized transaction state stay synchronized throughout execution. The work should ensure that event append, tx_state updates, and guarded workflow behavior remain aligned for tx.begin, verify, commit, and terminal transitions, preventing valid follow-up operations from failing against stale or incomplete state.
outputs: Runtime implementation that keeps tx_event_log and tx_state synchronized for canonical transaction transitions
Guard behavior that evaluates a consistent active transaction and verify/commit state
Clearer integrity handling for event append success followed by state update failure
acceptance_criteria: After tx.begin is emitted, subsequent task lifecycle operations recognize the same transaction as active without requiring manual recovery.
After verify events are emitted successfully, commit gating recognizes the transaction as verified from synchronized canonical state.
Canonical event append and tx_state persistence remain coupled strongly enough that later guarded operations do not observe stale lifecycle state.
The implementation prevents silent drift where last_applied_seq appears current but active_tx content reflects an older or empty state.
Integrity failures are surfaced explicitly when canonical event progression cannot be materialized into tx_state.
tasks: Review the current event append, state save, rebuild, and guard paths to identify where canonical state synchronization can be lost.
Implement synchronization changes so tx.begin, verify, commit, and terminal events materialize the expected tx_state before downstream guarded operations run.
Tighten integrity handling so event-log progress cannot silently outpace the persisted active transaction state.
Ensure task lifecycle tools, verify helpers, and commit helpers interpret the same active transaction and lifecycle position.
Document any required implementation constraints in code comments or helper boundaries where synchronization sequencing is critical.
notes: This ticket is the implementation counterpart to the Phase 1 contract for canonical synchronization.
Do not weaken workflow guards to mask synchronization bugs; fix the underlying state progression instead.
Treat event append and tx_state persistence as one canonical progression path wherever possible.
Use the observed 0.4.11 failure patterns as concrete guidance for what must no longer be possible after this ticket.
